portfolio_context.thesis: Auto-parts turnaround with operating leverage and margin recovery potential
portfolio_context.prior_review_notes: v4 (2026-03-07): HOLD. Debt refi completed Mar 4, 2026 ($1.1B at 9.25% due 2031). FY2025 adj. EBITDA margin 7.6%. Management guided 10%+ for 2026. Base case $110, CAGR 85.4%.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are catalysts showing up on schedule?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: Yes. Margin recovery is the core catalyst and is materializing: gross margin climbed from 3.7% (FY2021) to 11.7% (FY2025). Adj. EBITDA margin rose from effectively 0% (FY2022) to 7.6% (FY2025), with management guiding 10%+ for FY2026 ($260-300M on ~$2.8B revenue). Debt maturity wall eliminated via $1.1B refinancing to 2031. $298M new business awards secured in FY2025.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Is management saying one thing, doing another?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: No. FY2025 adj. EBITDA of $209.7M hit the high end of guidance. Third consecutive year of positive FCF ($16.3M). Debt refinancing executed as committed. $64M in plant efficiency cost savings delivered. Management credibility remains high under CEO Edwards (13+ years tenure).

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are margins shifting unexpectedly?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: No on a full-year basis. Q4 2025 saw a temporary dip (5.2% adj. EBITDA margin vs 8.2% prior year Q4) driven by Ford F-Series customer production disruptions -- not self-inflicted. Full-year trajectory remains strongly positive. Management expects Q1 2026 to be weakest quarter before improving through the year.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are competitors pulling ahead?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: No. Tier 2 auto parts peers (Dana, American Axle, Modine, Visteon) are not showing superior margin recovery trajectories. CPS's 74% EV/hybrid share of new business awards and China OEM pivot (targeting 60% of China revenue from Chinese OEMs by 2030, up from 36%) are differentiated positioning moves.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Which macro events actually matter to this business now?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: Three items: (a) 25% auto parts tariffs -- effective since May 2025. S&P forecasts CPS revenue growing 1.8% in 2026 despite tariffs; management stated net tariff effect on profitability expected to be zero. Global manufacturing footprint provides flexibility. (b) Auto production volumes -- S&P projects global production flat to -1% in 2026; Asia Pacific (+4.5%) offsets weakness. US light vehicle sales projected down to 15.6M from 16.1M. (c) Interest rates -- no longer a direct risk; debt is fixed at 9.25% through 2031.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: FY2025 adj. EBITDA margin 7.6% (up from ~4.3% FY2024). Guided 10%+ for 2026 ($260-300M). Gross margin 11.7%, targeting 13%+. $64M cost savings delivered.
Operating income $107M FY2025 vs $70M FY2024 (+53%). $298M new business awards. 99% product quality scorecard. Ford Supplier of the Year.
$1.1B at 9.25% due 2031 issued Mar 4, 2026. All 2026 and 2027 maturities fully redeemed. Net leverage target 2x by 2028. Net debt/EBITDA ~5.1x improving with EBITDA growth.
catalyst_tracking.verdict: Maturity wall eliminated. Deleveraging depends on EBITDA scaling.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: 74% of $298M new awards tied to BEV/hybrid. 51% of new awards with Chinese OEM customers. Asia Pacific growth at 4.5%. China OEM revenue share target: 36% to 60% by 2030.

management_consistency.what_management_said: Target margin expansion to 10%+ adj. EBITDA margin by 2026, mid-teens by 2030. Positive FCF. Refinance near-term debt. $250-300M new business award run rate. Sales target $3.8B by 2030. Triple ROIC by 2028.
management_consistency.what_management_did: Delivered $209.7M adj. EBITDA (high end of guidance). Positive FCF ($16.3M, third consecutive year). Completed $1.1B refinancing eliminating all 2026/2027 maturities. Secured $298M new business. Generated $64M cost savings. Revenue grew 0.4% to $2.74B (flat but on target).
management_consistency.match_mismatch: Match. All stated targets have been met or exceeded. Management credibility is high. CEO Edwards has been in role since 2012 with 39+ years of automotive industry experience.

margin_competitor_drift.margin_change: Gross and EBITDA margins continue expanding year-over-year. FCF margin is thin (0.6% FY2025) because ~$115M annual interest burden absorbs most operating cash flow. Post-refinancing, annual interest ~$102M ($1.1B at 9.25%). The real FCF unlock depends on EBITDA scaling: at 10% adj. EBITDA margin on $2.8B = $280M EBITDA minus ~$102M interest minus ~$55M capex = meaningful FCF generation in 2026-2027.
margin_competitor_drift.competitor_drift: No competitive loss observed. CPS margin recovery trajectory is above Tier 2 auto parts average. The EV/hybrid new business mix at 74% is a meaningful differentiator vs traditional ICE-focused peers.
open_risks: Leverage still elevated: $1.26B total debt, negative equity (-$83M), net debt/EBITDA ~5.1x -- but maturity wall eliminated through 2031
Interest expense ~$102M/yr at 9.25% limits FCF conversion until EBITDA scales further
Revenue has been flat at ~$2.7-2.8B for three years -- turnaround is margin-driven, needs volume/mix growth for full payoff
25% auto tariffs create ongoing uncertainty; US light vehicle sales projected down to 15.6M from 16.1M in 2026
Q4 2025 customer disruption (Ford F-Series) exposed single-program concentration risk
Negative equity means traditional valuation anchors (P/B) are unavailable

base_case_assumptions: Revenue: $3.0B -- management guided $2.7-2.9B for 2026 with ~3% growth; $298M new business awards support continued ramp to $3.0B by 2028
FCF margin: 5.5% -- at 10%+ EBITDA margin ($280M+) less ~$102M interest and tax effects, 5-6% FCF margin is achievable by 2028
Multiple: 12x -- cyclical/industrial baseline 14x, -2x for still-elevated leverage = 12x. Maturity wall eliminated supports this level.

worst_case_floor.summary: CPS has negative trough FCF margins in the 5-year history (FY2021: -9.1%, FY2022: -4.2%). Negative trough FCF margin produces a mechanically negative floor price, implying 100%+ downside. The business has moved substantially away from trough conditions: three consecutive years of positive FCF, EBITDA margin 7.6% and rising, debt refinanced to 2031.
worst_case_floor.downside_normalization: Mechanical floor is negative (-$94.22), implying >100% downside. Scored as 100% downside per scoring rules, capping position at 3%.

optional_scenarios.name: Conservative case
optional_scenarios.takeaway: Even with lower revenue ($2.8B), thinner margin (4.5%), and lower multiple (10x), the CAGR of 47.9% still clears the 30% hurdle comfortably.

sell_trigger_check.name: Sell trigger 1 (target reached / forward <30%)
sell_trigger_check.evidence: Forward CAGR 85.4%, well above 30% hurdle. Stock would need to reach ~$93 for forward returns to approach 15%.

sell_trigger_check.name: Sell trigger 2 (rapid move / forward <10-15%)
sell_trigger_check.evidence: At $32, price is far below any trigger level. Rapid-move guardrail would activate around $93+.

sell_trigger_check.name: Sell trigger 3 (fundamental thesis break)
sell_trigger_check.evidence: Thesis is strengthening. Margin recovery on track. Debt refinanced through 2031. Management executing on all commitments. EV/hybrid positioning advancing. Tariff impact assessed as 'muted' by management and manageable per S&P analysis.

holding_verdict.reason: The turnaround thesis is intact and has strengthened since the original purchase. The critical debt maturity wall was eliminated via $1.1B refinancing to 2031. FY2025 delivered the third consecutive year of positive FCF, adj. EBITDA margin improved to 7.6% with management guiding 10%+ for 2026, and all stated targets were met or exceeded. At $32, the base case forward CAGR of 85.4% to a $110 target provides a large margin of safety above the 30% hurdle, and even the conservative case ($70 target) clears at 47.9%. No sell triggers are active.
monitor_items: Q1 2026 margin recovery -- confirm bounce-back from Q4 2025 customer disruption (5.2% adj. EBITDA margin)
2026 EBITDA margin trajectory -- management guided 10%+; need quarterly progression toward $260-300M range
Revenue growth -- needs $2.9B+ in 2026 to support $3.0B by 2028; $298M new business awards pipeline should help
Tariff/trade developments -- 25% auto tariffs; US light vehicle sales projected down to 15.6M from 16.1M
Interest burden -- ~$102M/yr at 9.25% is manageable but limits FCF conversion until EBITDA scales

sources: Cooper Standard Debt Refinancing Press Release (Mar 4, 2026)
S&P Global Ratings CPS Credit Analysis (Mar 2026)
